methodology: GW38 is the final gameweek of the 2024-25 PL season. All 10 fixtures kick off simultaneously. FDR ratings 1-5 (1=easiest). Away venue adds +0.3 weighted penalty. No multi-GW window applicable — single-fixture GW. Rotation risk flagged separately for teams with nothing to play for.

fixtures.notes: Arsenal host Everton (relegated). Gunners have strong clean sheet potential. Everton have nothing to play for, likely to rotate.
Bournemouth at home to mid-table Palace. Good fixture for Bournemouth assets. Palace away is moderate.
Brentford host Man City. Hard home fixture for Brentford. Man City away at Brentford is manageable — but City rotation risk is elevated if title race is settled.
Brighton host a struggling Man Utd. Easy home game for Brighton assets. Man Utd away is tough given poor season form.
Balanced clash between two solid mid/upper-table sides. Neither team enjoys a clear fixture advantage.
Fulham host a relegated/struggling Wolves. Excellent attacking fixture for Fulham. Wolves away at Fulham is extremely tough — Wolves have nothing to play for.
Liverpool away at Ipswich — the standout fixture of GW38. Ipswich facing Liverpool is as hard as it gets. Liverpool assets (Salah, Diaz) are prime captaincy picks.
Both teams likely already relegated. Low-quality fixture. Avoid assets from both clubs due to low motivation and rotation risk.
Competitive top-half fixture. Both sides likely chasing European places. Balanced difficulty — quality assets on both sides can deliver.
Spurs host West Ham. Good home fixture for Tottenham attackers. West Ham away at Spurs is a moderate ask.

fixture_ratings.verdict: BEST — FDR 1 away at relegated Ipswich. Salah/Diaz are top captaincy options.
EXCELLENT — home FDR 2 vs relegated Everton. Strong clean sheet and attacking potential.
EXCELLENT — home FDR 2 vs relegated/poor Wolves. Ideal for budget Fulham attackers.
EXCELLENT — home FDR 2. Man Utd have had a dreadful season. Brighton attack should fire.
GOOD — home FDR 2 vs Palace. Reliable fixture for Bournemouth assets.
GOOD — home FDR 2 vs West Ham. Good for Spurs attackers but beware rotation if European place is secured.
GOOD — FDR 2 away but City heavily rotate on final day. Haaland start not guaranteed.
LOW APPEAL despite FDR 2 — both relegated teams, motivation rock bottom. Avoid.
NEUTRAL — FDR 3 away. Palace assets are moderate picks.
NEUTRAL — FDR 3 away at a strong Newcastle. Villa attackers still viable if chasing European spots.
NEUTRAL — FDR 3 at home. Newcastle home record is strong but Villa are a real test.
NEUTRAL — FDR 3. Chelsea inconsistent at home this season; Forest solid away.
NEUTRAL/AVOID — FDR 3 away at Stamford Bridge. Forest attackers niche picks only.
AVOID — relegated team, zero motivation regardless of easy fixture on paper.
AVOID — FDR 3 away at Spurs. West Ham had a difficult season.
BAD — FDR 4 away at Brighton who are in form at home. Avoid Utd assets.
BAD — hosting Man City. Brentford defenders and attackers face a hostile FDR 4 fixture.
WORST — relegated, no motivation, travelling to Arsenal. Avoid all Everton assets.
WORST — relegated/struggling, away at Fulham. Worst fixture of the GW for outfield assets.
WORST — hosting Liverpool. Ipswich defenders/GK face an onslaught. Avoid entirely.

best_fixtures: Liverpool (away at Ipswich — FDR 1, prime captaincy pick, Salah/Diaz)
Arsenal (home vs Everton — FDR 2, clean sheet + attacking potential)
Fulham (home vs Wolves — FDR 2, best budget attacking fixture)
Brighton (home vs Man Utd — FDR 2, Man Utd worst away form)
Bournemouth (home vs Crystal Palace — FDR 2, reliable home fixture)
Tottenham (home vs West Ham — FDR 2, good for Son/Solanke type assets)
worst_fixtures: Everton (away at Arsenal — FDR 5 + relegated, total avoidance)
Wolves (away at Fulham — FDR 5 + relegated, total avoidance)
Ipswich (home vs Liverpool — FDR 5, facing champions/title-chasers)
Man Utd (away at Brighton — FDR 4 + poor form)
Brentford (home vs Man City — FDR 4, hosting title-challengers)
rotation_warnings: Man City: heavy rotation expected if title position is settled before final day. Haaland rest is plausible.
Everton: all-out rotation as a relegated side with nothing to play for.
Wolves: similar motivation vacuum — likely heavy squad rotation.
Leicester / Southampton: both relegated, rotation expected.
Any European-qualified team (Arsenal, Spurs, Newcastle, Aston Villa) may rest key players if CL/EL spots are locked in.
captaincy_recommendation: M.Salah (Liverpool) — FDR 1 away at Ipswich is the best captaincy fixture in GW38. Strong differential alternative: Joao Pedro (Brighton) or a Fulham attacker home vs Wolves.
best_def_run: Arsenal home vs Everton — strongest clean sheet probability of the GW. Secondary: Fulham home vs Wolves.
notes: GW38 is a simultaneous single-day event (25 May 2025) — all 10 fixtures at the same kick-off time.
No DGW or BGW teams confirmed for GW38. Every team plays exactly once.
FDR scale: 1=very easy, 2=easy, 3=medium, 4=hard, 5=very hard. Away penalty of +0.3 applied to weighted score.
Rotation risk is the biggest concern for GW38 transfers — confirm team news before deadline.
Chip usage (Triple Captain, Bench Boost) commonly deployed in GW38 — factor this into captaincy weighting.
summary: GW38 has no DGW/BGW complications. Liverpool away at Ipswich (FDR 1) is the premier fixture. Arsenal, Fulham, Brighton and Bournemouth all offer FDR 2 home games. Everton, Wolves and Ipswich face the three hardest fixtures. Rotation risk is the primary wild card — deadline-day team news is essential.
